title: Buildah will try to push via HTTP even if --tls-verify option is used; there is also no option to force https only usage
body:
Our registry is only accessible via HTTPS but there is no option to tell buildah that, so we get errors like
```
bot@ops-slave1:~/workspace/container-ops/debian-php7.4$ buildah push --tls-verify debian-php7.4 docker://example.com/php7.4-fcgi
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 1d11db3eaba6 [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 138.4MiB
Copying blob d925e0fae4e6 [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 123.4MiB
WARN[0031] Failed, retrying in 2s ... (1/3). Error: writing blob: Patch "http://example.com/v2/php7.4-fcgi/blobs/uploads/fb6992fd-bed0-470a-ba98-0d7553243d1f?_state=R-O0bSDYnm65kHL0tSChzV0Not-FYDJQUlKe8f9HyEp7Ik5hbWUiOiJwaHA3LjQtZmNnaSIsIlVVSUQiOiJmYjY5OTJmZC1iZWQwLTQ3MGEtYmE5OC0wZDc1NTMyNDNkMWYiLCJPZmZzZXQiOjAsIlN0YXJ0ZWRBdCI6IjIwMjMtMDUtMTFUMTQ6MTU6NTIuNzAzMjMyMTE0WiJ9": dial tcp 10.0.0.10:80: i/o timeout 
```

Ive tried adding `:443`  but there doesn't seem to be a way to tell it "use HTTPS only"

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**
```
apt list buildah
Listing... Done
buildah/unstable,now 1.28.2+ds1-3 amd64 [installed]
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
buildah version
Version:         1.28.2
Go Version:      go1.19.8
Image Spec:      1.1.0-rc2
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.23.1
Git Commit:      
Built:           Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
(paste your output here)
```

```

comments.body: @XANi `--tls-verify` is by default true in buildah I don't think you need to do anything. What happens when you do `buildah push debian-php7.4 example.com/php7.4-fcgi`

Exactly same thing happens, I tried that method at first, then I tried to force TLS via that flag

.Notice that mistake with `--tls-verify` is *trying unencrypted 80 in the first place* as it is entirely pointless. So kind of 2 different bugs:

* inability to point at HTTPS only server
* `--tls-verify` tries port 80 first.

The config of server itself might be a bit unusual, as the port 80 is not listening at all (blocked on firewall) vs the usual setup where port 80 redirects to 443. In our setup we basically blocked port 80 so nobody tries to send unencrypted by accident.

If I do same on server that has both 80 and 443 open it works.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@mtrmac @vrothberg PTAL

Is this a problem in containers/image?

c/image only ever tries unencrypted HTTP if HTTPS fails.

Please collect debug-level logs (`buildah --log-level=debug …`), for a start.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Closing; @XANi please reopen when more information becomes available.

---

More details:
- The fallback from HTTPS to HTTP only ever happens if a “ping“ to `/v2/` over `https` fails, and in that case we require a `http://…/v2/` ping to succeed. So, assuming connections to `:80` always hang and timeout, that ping wouldn’t have succeeded either. So we are _not_ dealing with a fallback to HTTP in any ordinary sense
- A `Patch "http:/example…` with a single slash, not `//`, is not something the HTTP code could produce AFAICS
- So my best guess is that this is because the upload itself is encountering a HTTP redirect, or something like that. But that’s impossible to diagnose from the information above.

So, closing because there is no more data.

The problem appears to be the fact that checks whether a given blob is already on server is running on :443, but try to upload the data tries :80 (and fallbacks incorrectly)

And sheer fact that it TRIES :80 when I explicitly asked for `--tls-verify` is a bug

> The fallback from HTTPS to HTTP only ever happens if a “ping“ to /v2/ over https fails, and in that case we require a http://…/v2/ ping to succeed. So, assuming connections to :80 always hang and timeout, that ping wouldn’t have succeeded either. So we are not dealing with a fallback to HTTP in any ordinary sense

The repository was only available on 443. I want it to try HTTPS, don't try HTTP in the first place, that is the problem, there is no way to force using HTTPS only (which probably in itself is a security flaw)

If you look closer you'd see that the first part ( "Getting image source signatures") that checks whether signatures are on the server succeeded (over :443) but later part (actually uploading them) tries :80 and fails

It only tries pushing image data on :80 for some reason. If I push image that already exists on server it works fine.

> A Patch "http:/example… with a single slash, not //, is not something the HTTP code could produce AFAICS

I made a typo anonymizing URL, I fixed it.

> So my best guess is that this is because the upload itself is encountering a HTTP redirect, or something like that. But that’s impossible to diagnose from the information above.

As I already wrote registry is only accessible on :443. The port :80 does not answer on any packets, it's filtered on firewall i the first place. 

It says so in the error message: `dial tcp 10.0.0.10:80: i/o timeout` as in "it tried to connect to port 80, it failed to connect to port 80, it didn't try 443 AT ALL". 

I have no idea what more info you need.

Just block port 80 on firewall (`iptables -I OUTPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j DROP`) and run push at the registry. It's extremely easy to reproduce.

Here is how push looks to registry with only :443 accessible:
```
Getting image source signatures 
Copying blob 934aad3e9742 [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 138.4MiB
Copying blob 3dec696a3faa [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 123.4MiB
WARN[0030] Failed, retrying in 2s ... (1/3). Error: writing blob: Patch "http://example.com/php7.4-fcgi/blobs/uploads/e540f063-4278-466a-b427-234f32c19d94?_state=p4RvSQEdgI0TmMaje3dFr5D9dKyTBCtRe5hUkMmpxil7Ik5hbWUiOiJwaHA3LjQtZmNnaSIsIlVVSUQiOiJlNTQwZjA2My00Mjc4LTQ2NmEtYjQyNy0yMzRmMzJjMTlkOTQiLCJPZmZzZXQiOjAsIlN0YXJ0ZWRBdCI6IjIwMjMtMDctMTVUMjM6MTM6NDAuNDE2NTg2ODc2WiJ9": dial tcp 10.0.2.146:80: i/o timeout 
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 934aad3e9742 [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 138.4MiB
Copying blob 3dec696a3faa [--------------------------------------] 8.0b / 123.4MiB
WARN[0063] Failed, retrying in 2s ... (2/3). Error: writing blob: Patch "http://hq-dockerhub.non.3dart.com/v2/php7.4-fcgi/blobs/uploads/cc657cd4-7d41-432c-bd05-5ef33a38c1be?_state=D5P6Hq9SFnifRvYSnP0wPIj8jLGPDeYrSa6LvpvgaKV7Ik5hbWUiOiJwaHA3LjQtZmNnaSIsIlVVSUQiOiJjYzY1N2NkNC03ZDQxLTQzMmMtYmQwNS01ZWYzM2EzOGMxYmUiLCJPZmZzZXQiOjAsIlN0YXJ0ZWRBdCI6IjIwMjMtMDctMTVUMjM6MTQ6MTIuODQwNjc1MzQzWiJ9": dial tcp 10.0.2.146:80: i/o timeout 
Getting image source signatures
```

Here is same thing to registry which has both accessible:

```
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 3dec696a3faa done  
Copying blob 934aad3e9742 done  
Copying config 5beca3945b done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

And here is a try for a repo that already has the blobs loaded:

```
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 6798740a49ea skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 03f35b7f38b9 skipped: already exists  
Copying config 5beca3945b done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```

It only fails when:

* port :80 is blocked (`iptables -I OUTPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j DROP`)
* there is actual data to push (checks for whether blobs already exist on registry use HTTPS correctly)

> @XANi please reopen when more information becomes available.

@mtrmac I don't have permissions to do so

> I have no idea what more info you need.

That’s
> Please collect debug-level logs (`buildah --log-level=debug …`), for a start.

---

> It only fails when:

…
> there is actual data to push (checks for whether blobs already exist on registry use HTTPS correctly)

Because the HTTPS/HTTP fallback gets decided _once_ per push operation, at the start, that suggests that the push code is _not_ falling back.

AFAICS the way this could happen is if the registry, in its https://github.com/distribution/distribution/blob/main/docs/spec/api.md#uploading-the-layer operation, returns a `Location:` header pointing to the `:80` port. That would be a misconfiguration of the registry, maybe something related to https://github.com/distribution/distribution/blob/003dd5aaa1f37ae25dc55bbad01ae1b987184044/registry/handlers/app.go#L808C9-L815 (missing/incorrect `HTTP.Host`, or a proxy sending incorrect …forwarded… headers.)

Huh, I think you're onto something. We're using standard docker registry containers behind a loadbalancer (that does https) and it does send a location header, and for some reason entire URL instead of just the path

```
POST /v2/php7.4-fcgi/blobs/uploads/ HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com
User-Agent: Buildah/1.28.2
Content-Length: 0
Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0
Accept-Encoding: gzip
Connection: close
X-Forwarded-For: 10.100.153.11

HTTP/1.1 202 Accepted
Content-Length: 0
Docker-Distribution-Api-Version: registry/2.0
Docker-Upload-Uuid: e7065a70-57d3-40ad-8ba3-beca8dcde2c1
Location: http://example.com/v2/php7.4-fcgi/blobs/uploads/e7065a70-57d3-40ad-8ba3-beca8dcde2c1?_state=oYRjq9ndfnXbOrVbXoEWytk4XsL2QqviVVqLmko_Ub57Ik5hbWUiOiJwaHA3LjQtZmNnaSIsIlVVSUQiOiJlNzA2NWE3MC01N2QzLTQwYWQtOGJhMy1iZWNhOGRjZGUyYzEiLCJPZmZzZXQiOjAsIlN0YXJ0ZWRBdCI6IjIwMjMtMDctMTdUMTQ6NDc6NTguMjc0NDcyMzIxWiJ9
Range: 0-0
X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
Date: Mon, 17 Jul 2023 14:47:58 GMT
Connection: close
```

The weirder part is that other tools we used didn't complain about it... anyway, thanks for help :)
